name: Stir Fried Bok Choy with Tofu
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Soft tofu, cut into 1/2-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice bok choy into bite-sized pieces, separating the green and white parts.
2. In a bowl, combine water, shoyu, and 5-spice powder.
3. Add tofu to the marinade and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
4. Remove the tofu from the marinade and place on an oiled baking sheet.
5. Bake the tofu at 375°F (190°C) for 20 minutes on one side, then turn and bake for another 10 minutes on the other side.
6. Dissolve kuzu or arrowroot in 1/4 cup of the tofu marinade.
7. Heat sesame seed oil in a wok or large pot.
8. Add the white parts of the bok choy, cover, and steam over reduced heat for 5 minutes.
9. Uncover and add the rest of the bok choy.
10. Stir the kuzu or arrowroot mixture and gradually pour it into the bok choy liquid over medium heat.
11. Serve with toasted tofu.
Per serving: 60 calories, 3 g fat
From: Annemarie Colbin, author of "The Natural Gourment"
